characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Apprentice: All the child characters are apprentices learning a trade from their mentors, such as cooking or dancing. Cartoon Creature: All the character designs in this show are very wacky and silly, and most of them look either like animals or cartoonish monsters with no definitive species. Edible Theme Naming: In a show about cooking, every character is named after a kind of food. Invisible Parents: None of the apprentices' parents are ever seen nor mentioned. Masculine Girl, Feminine Boy:Married couple, Truffles and Mung Daal, seem to have shades of this dynamic. Truffles has shown to be the most dominant person at the Catering Company, she's very aggressive,short-tempered, brash and incredibly strongdespite her size. Mung on the other hand is far more sensitive, caring, soft-spoken, hascross dressed several timesin the series and has shown signs of beingAmbiguously BiandIn Touch with His Feminine Side.There is also Panini, who is aClingy Jealous Girlwith a fiery aggressive side when it comes time trying to get close to Chowder and Ceviche, who is so sweet and sensitive that even hisappearance is feminine.

characters.name: Chowder
characters.description: Chowder is an excitable, youngcat...bear...rabbit...thingyand apprentice of legendary chef Mung Daal. Chowder loves food and dreams of becoming a great chef one day. Affectionate Nickname: Panini calls him "Num-nums". The Apprentice: To Mung. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: If there's anything involving a tasty snack, he'll become distracted. Actually, even the tiniest most insignificant thing can cause him to completely lose focus. Ax-Crazy: In the beginning of the episode "The Poltergeist".

characters.name: Mung Daal
characters.description: He can be a bit scatterbrained, but when it comes to cooking, Mung Daal knows more than almost anyone. He pours his heart into every dish. Mung thinks he's a ladies man and frequently offers Chowder advice about finding love. Abandoned Catchphrase: Mung used to say "Sweet beans in a basket!" in the first trailer and pilot, but it wasn't kept past that point. Amazing Technicolour Population: If you thought he was blue enough, he was a bright teal in his childhood form. Ambiguously Bi: He's been married to Truffles for hundreds of years and flirts with the ladies, but there's a surprisingly high number of homoerotic moments between him and Shnitzel. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: For all their bickering and arguing, “Mung On The Rocks” shows that Mung truly does love Truffles and is a miserable wreck without her in his life.

characters.name: Shnitzel
characters.description: Shnitzel is a big, heavy rock monster (and yes, "rock monster" isthe exact term used in-universe) who works in Mung Daal's kitchen. He's a serious fellow who tends to end up cleaning up his co-workers' messes. Angrish: According to one episode, he talks exclusively in this and, when he's able to be relaxed, he actually speaks more clearly but he reverts back to this. Berserk Button: Donotcall him lazy. He talked back to Truffles when she did so and nearly left her at aninsane criminal's mercy for it. The Big Guy: He's the biggest member in Mung Daal's kitchen and usually does the heavy lifting. Butt-Monkey: Nothing ever seems to go right for him. Any work in the kitchen usually involves him getting hurt or doing something he doesn't particularly enjoy.

characters.name: Truffles
characters.description: Mung's wife, Truffles likes to be the boss and has a very competitive streak. While Mung handles the cooking, Truffles takes care of everything else. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Despite their arguments, she and Mung clearly show love to each other, with Truffles defending her husband's cooking despiteinsulting it at times herself. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Mung, which is odd, considering they've already been married for centuries and thus would have no reason to hide it. Berserk Button: There are rules to follow in order to avoid her wrath.If you bring gum home, youmustshare with her, or ELSE.Neverinterrupt her mahjong game.Leave her money box alone.Only Truffles canpick on Mung Daal and his cooking; she severs ties with the Big Hat Biddies when the leader insults Mung, his cooking, and Chowder. If you bring gum home, youmustshare with her, or ELSE.

characters.name: Kimchi
characters.description: Chowder's pet, who lives in a cage next to his bed. Kimchi is a brown-colored cloud (an anthropomorphized flatus). He likes things that smell bad, and he "talks" by making flatulent sounds. Creator Cameo: He is "voiced" by C. H. Greenblatt. Demoted to Extra: By the time season 2 rolled around, he barely shows up. Dub Name Change: In Japan, he is renamed "Natto" (a fermented soybean dish that is very smelly), possibly toavoid having a character with a Korean name. Meaningful Name: Kimchi is a Korean dish made with fermented vegetables, which has a rather strong smell.

characters.name: Ms. Endive
characters.description: A female chef who teaches cooking to Panini with strict discipline. She regularly berates Mung Daal, whom she considers a rival. In the episode "Chowder's Girlfriend", it is revealed she despises boys and boyfriends because her fiance did not show up on their wedding day. The creator describes her as Martha Stewart with Oompa-Loompa colors. Abhorrent Admirer: To Shnitzel in for most of the series. Hereallyisn't very fond of her advances towards him. She sometimes shows some of this to Mung if they're alone together, to an equal amount of disgust. Acrofatic: Despite her size, she can move quite quickly if she wanted to. Berserk Button:Nevertry to peek on her in the shower, willingly or not. "Chowder's Babysitter" had her catch Chowder and Gazpacho in her bathroom andchase them down furiously for revenge. Hermassivesize increaseduring this point didn't help.Endive:I'll teach you to spy on a beautiful woman! Big Eater: While unmatched by Chowder's gluttony, she's definitely prone to stuffing her facein a disgusting fashion.

characters.name: Panini
characters.description: Panini is the apprentice to Ms. Endive. She's convinced that Chowder is her boyfriend and takes every opportunity to let him know this. 10-Minute Retirement: Every time she "breaks up" with Chowder, it boils down to this. The first time she does this inChowder's Girlfriend, she goes back to obsessing over Chowder in her next appearance. Abhorrent Admirer: To Chowder, to the point that her touchaloneis enough to cause his skin to burn, howeverthis becomes averted in the series finale.Chowder: Hey, Panini, guess what?Panini: You love me?Chowder: Ew. No. Berserk Button: Anyone and anything that gets between her and her attempts to claim Chowder, even Chowder himself, will see she's not as cute as she looks. Beware the Nice Ones: She's cute at first glance, but she's not one to be crossed. She once violently forced Chowder to hold her hand and she almost beat Gorgonzola into a pulp after he kicked her in a misunderstanding.

characters.name: Gazpacho
characters.description: An anthropomorphic woolly mammoth storekeeper who sells strange produce and ingredients. He does his best to offer advice to Chowder when needed. He lives with his overbearing mother, who never appears (and at several points is implied to not evenexist). Gazpacho shows no interest in moving out and improving his own life, and his grasp on his sanity is... tenuous at best, which can lead to occasional moments of psychosis and paranoia, often with disastrious consequences to the episode's plot. Abusive Parents: From what we hear about his mom, she'sverycontrolling of him; he once stated that she's "usually" mad at him, and that she "crushes hopes and dreams." Affectionate Parody/Deconstructive Parody: There is one episode where he dresses up as his mother and is playing it as a funny parody of Norman Bates from the Alfred Hitchcock moviePsycho. However, this may also work as adark deconstructionof his character. AManchildwho was constantly put down by his mother to a point where it messes with his development and even his sanity could go down the same insane path as Norman Bates. Ascended Extra: Initially more of side character, Gazpacho started to gain more prominence as the show progressed, to the point he even had his own episode, titledGazpacho!. Ax-Crazy: He's essentially a nicer and (somewhat) kid-friendlier version ofMaster Shake(ironically,another characterthatDana Snydervoices).In that episode he gives an offhand comment that he is going to kill Chowder, Gorgonzola and Panini after coffee. That's right, he's going to stabthree children, featuring his best friend.In "Dinner Theatre", he enjoys his villainous role a little too much, pretending to be this character outside practicing and at the end this line occurs with literal axe craziness:Chowder:Well, at least no one died.Gazpacho:Well, at least...[takes an axe]NOT! YET!!![cueDramatic Thunder]"Banned From The Stand" has Gazpacho going paranoid that Mung and Chowder are going to buy berries off of him by help

characters.name: Gorgonzola
characters.description: A young apprentice candle holder with a surly attitude. He resents Chowder due to him having a better job. Early-Bird Cameo: Like Gazpacho, Gorgonzola first appears in the pilot without any lines, but unlike the former, he's shown glaring at Chowder while he's on his way home, giving a hint to what their relationship is like. Foil: To Chowder. Chowder is a wide-eyedCheerful Childwho loves his job as Mung's apprentice, while Gorgonzola is a cynicalJerkasswho hates his job as Stilton's apprentice because of his terrible home life. Going hand-in-hand with this, Chowder resembles a cat (a cat-bear-rabbit thing, anyway) while Gorgonzola isa rat. Friendly Enemy: In "Panini for President" and "The Blackout". Future Badass: Played with. As an adult, he goes from lowly candle holder to the wealthy CEO of his own company, but he's stillshort for his age (and clearly insecure about it)and adult Chowder remains too sweet and scatterbrained to be jealous of Gorgonzola being more successful than him, much to Gorgonzola's chagrin.

characters.name: Ceviché
characters.description: Ceviché is a dancer and the apprentice of Pate. He is fairly easygoing and has a crush on Panini. Because of this, he follows her around and does what she says. Abhorrent Admirer: To Panini, who’s disgusted by his crush on her since she’s in love with Chowder. Amazing Technicolor Hair: He has purple hair. Though in "A Faire to Remember", it’s revealed his hair is actually a wig. Baldness Angst: In "A Faire to Remember", it’s revealed his hair is just a wig he wears to hide his baldness. Black Bead Eyes: Has tiny black dots for eyes. However, in "The Dinner Theater", there is sclera around them in a close-up of his face.

characters.name: Pate
characters.description: Ceviche's master and an expert dancer, Pate is an athletic humanoid and one of the less neurotic and dyfunctional artisans of Marzipan City. Ambiguously Human: Pate looks like an athletic, Caucasian man, with his only weird traits being his bulbous nose, huge lips, and green hair. Cool Teacher: He's much nicer to Ceviche than Mung, Endive andespeciallyStilton are to their apprentices. Edible Theme Naming: Named for a French meat paste. Gag Lips: He has huge lips.

characters.name: Chestnut
characters.description: A tiny, blue, gremlin-like creature who's somewhat greedy and likes making mundane objects into his personal home décor. Baritone of Strength: He's got one of the deepest voices in the entire show, and he's a lot stronger than he looks. Berserk Button: Donotmess with anything Chestnut has declared his property. Cloudcuckoolander: His first appearance involves him waving his arms around while sitting in a puddle in the street and singing about himself. He also repeatedly identifies objects incorrectly—such as calling a stop sign a sofa, Chowder's hat a vacation home, and a passed-out Mung Daal as a dining room table—and immediately claims them as his own. It's very clear there's something not quite right with him. Dark Horse Victory: In "Hands on a Big Mixer", after Chowder thinks he's won the titular prize, it's revealed that Chestnut was holding onto it the whole time and nobody noticed because of how tiny he is, sohegets the mixer.Chestnut:Dink-a-loo, dink-a-lee, Chestnut gets this thing for free.

characters.name: Mr. Fugu & Foie Gras
characters.description: A red balloon who is Mung's best customer, and his cat valet. Mr. Fugu is known to be an absolute glutton who never lets his valet have any food. Big Eater: His appetite is bigger than Chowder's. Any time he's presented with food, he orders a mountain load, and relentlessly wolfs down on it. Control Freak: When it comes to his birthday parties, he's very tight-laced about the themes he has for them, to the point of ruling his parties with an iron fist (as in, having the attendantspunch each other with iron fists). Entitled Bastard: He always feels entitled to eat before his valet, and never leaves anything left for them. Extreme Omnivore: He'll eat anything, from tacos, to Thrice Cream, to clouds, tobird vomit.

characters.name: Reuben
characters.description: A porcine con man who always antagonizes Mung Daal and company whenever he has a major appearance. Bad Review Threat: In "The Rat Sandwich", he hides his rat in the sandwich Mung serves him in order toblackmail the Mung Daal Catering company into catering to his every whim and serving him free food. Casting Gag: His name (Reuben) is a joking reference to the name of his voice actor: Paul Reubens. Con Man: He performs various jobs mainly to manipulate people and swindle them. Dirty Coward: Once it's revealed in "The Party Cruise" that he stole the titular party cruise and the real owners arrive to get it back, Reuben immediately abandons ship and leaves everyone onboard to get arrested just to save himself.

characters.name: The Meaches
characters.description: Angry Guard Dog: The Meach Guards act exactly this way, complete with barks and snarls, though they're capable of speech if they want to. Fantastic Fruits and Vegetables: A species of living, pumpkin-sized peaches, though only the Queen Meach is considered edible. King Mook: The Queen Meach looks exactly like her guards, aside from having a feminine voice and lips, as well as a princess hat. Let's Meet the Meat: The Queen Meach doesn't particularly seem to mind being taken away and used for food, and mostly seems to be amused by the whole thing. More Teeth than the Osmond Family: The Meaches have no limbs, which they compensate for with a mouth full of giant fangs.

characters.name: Kiwi
characters.description: Character Tic: He usually punctuates a sentence by flapping his arms. Greek Chorus: He is usually seen making fun of or encouraging other characters, or simply commentating on a scene. Messy Maggots: Kiwi always has flies buzzing around him. Nice Guy: Though he likes to snark a little, this guy is very laid-back encouraging.

characters.name: Gumbo
characters.description: Mung Daal's apprentice before Chowder came along, who seeks revenge on Mung for wounding his pride. Big Bad Wannabe: Heshouldbe the main antagonist of "The Deadly Maze", but he starts off the episode accidentally trapping himself in his maze with Chowder and forced to travel with him to escape. The fact that's he paired with Chowder means a tough time for the poor guy to say the least to the point it's quite easy to sympathize with him, and by the end he just gives up to cut his losses. Also, antagonists likeEndiveandReubenhave personal animosity with Mung to help sell their roles, whereas Gumbo is merely another apprentice for Mung who just walked out on him in a rage. Disproportionate Retribution: He's been plotting against Mung for a long time because the latter... gently pointed out he used too much Kooky Wooky spice for his Toiled Schmegg, which hurt his pride. Even Evil Has Standards: He admits that Chowder working for Mung is much more painful for the man than his own scheme. Evil Counterpart: To Chowder; both are known as Mung's apprentices and want to impress him with their efforts. Chowder, however, genuinely loves Mung as a surrogate father thick and thin and respects his abilities, while Gumbo walked out on Mung the moment he tried to help him and spent a good chunk of his life plotting revenge...over a dish Mung offered constructive criticism. Their ressemblance is evenlampshadedby Chowder, who initially mistakes him for his brother.
